market_notes: Tech correction entering second week (QQQ -3.4%, SMH -2.7% 1W) with persistent flight-to-quality: ARM +8.9%, MRVL +7.9%, TSEM +5.1%, CIEN +4.6% outperforming while mega-cap mega-growth remains weak (MSFT -6.6%, GOOG -8.4%, NVDA -3.0%, LLY -3.1%). Market is repricing growth expectations downward but maintaining confidence in structural AI capex cycle—semiconductor equipment/IP licensing/networking infrastructure names remain bid despite broad selloff. Macro backdrop uncertain: Fed guidance, recession risk signals, but enterprise semiconductor orders and foundry demand remain resilient. Portfolio positioned in high-quality infrastructure beneficiaries with catalysts; no urgency to chase amid uncertainty.
watching: ARM earnings guidance on AI/CPU IP licensing momentum—key validation of infrastructure thesis
TSEM/MRVL technical breakouts ($175, $95 resistance levels)—confirm continuation of relative strength
Semiconductor equipment orders data (KLAC, ASML guidance) for AI capex cycle confirmation
META short stop loss $562.52—reassess on relief bounce; legal headwinds remain structural
VIX technicals and SPY support ($580-600 range)—monitor for macro capitulation vs. grinding lower
Federal Reserve communications for rate guidance and recession risk signaling
NVIDIA/AI announcements as primary category catalyst for rotation acceleration

recent_runs.reasoning: Market is in a pronounced tech selloff with SPY down 2.2% WoW and QQQ down 3.4%, yet ARM has outperformed (+8.9% 1W) on quality rotation themes while META shows capitulation (-11.4% 1W, vol ratio 2.02). Risk/reward is asymmetric: ARM's thesis remains intact with strong momentum and relative strength; META's short is now deeply underwater on a panic move. Holding 91% cash with only two tiny positions suggests underdeployment—looking for high-conviction entries in oversold, quality names with catalysts ahead.
